assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: The Company covenants and agrees that, between the date of this Agreement and the earlier of the Effective Time and the date, if any, on which this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1, except (i) as may be required by Law, (ii) as may be agreed in writing by Parent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, delayed or conditioned), (iii) as may be expressly contemplated or required pursuant to this Agreement or (iv) as set forth on Section 5.1 of the Company Disclosure Letter, (A) the Company shall, and shall cause its Subsidiaries to, use commercially reasonable efforts to conduct the business of the Company and its Subsidiaries in the ordinary course of business and, to the extent consistent therewith, use commercially reasonable efforts to (x) preserve its assets and business organization and maintain its existing relationships with material customers, suppliers, distributors, Governmental Authorities and business partners, and (y) keep available the services of key employees and comply in all material respects with applicable Law with respect to employee hiring practices and promotion practices, and (B) the Company shall not, and shall cause its Subsidiaries not to, directly or indirectly:
assessed_clauses.processed_text: The the Company covenants and agrees that, between the date of this Agreement and the earlier of the Effective Time and the date, if any, on which this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1, except (i) as may be required by Law, (ii) as may be agreed in writing by Parent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, delayed or conditioned), (iii) as may be expressly contemplated or required pursuant to this Agreement or (iv) as set forth on Section 5.1 of the Company Disclosure Letter, (A) the Company shall, and shall cause its Subsidiaries to, use commercially reasonable efforts to conduct the business of the Company and its Subsidiaries in the ordinary course of business and, to the extent consistent therewith, use commercially reasonable efforts to (x) preserve its assets and business organization and maintain its existing relationships with material customers, suppliers, distributors, Governmental Authorities and business partners, and (y) keep available the services of key employees and comply in all material respects with applicable Law with respect to employee hiring practices and promotion practices, and (B) the Company shall not, and shall cause its Subsidiaries not to, directly or indirectly:
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Pre-Closing Conduct of Business and Operations Restrictions
seller_favorable_aspects: Carveouts for actions required by law
Consent from Parent cannot be unreasonably withheld
red_flags: Potentially vague terms like 'commercially reasonable efforts'
Broad restrictions on business conduct may limit operational flexibility
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from deviating from ordinary business practices and requires it to maintain relationships and preserve assets pending the merger.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (a) amend (i) the Company's Certificate of Incorporation, (ii) the Bylaws or (iii) such equivalent organizational or governing documents of any of its Subsidiaries, in the case of such documents of any of its Subsidiaries, in a manner that would be materially adverse to Parent or Merger Sub or would, or would reasonably be expected to, have the effect of delaying or preventing the consummation of the Merger or the other transactions contemplated by this Agreement;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (a) amend (i) the Company's Certificate of Incorporation, (ii) the Bylaws or (iii) such equivalent organizational or governing documents of any of its Subsidiaries, in the case of such documents of any of its Subsidiaries, in a manner that would be materially adverse to Parent or Merger Sub or would, or would reasonably be expected to, have the effect of delaying or preventing the consummation of the Merger or the other transactions contemplated by this Agreement;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Restrictions on Amending Organizational and Governing Documents
seller_favorable_aspects: Material adverse effect threshold
Focus on preventing delays rather than outright prohibitions
red_flags: Potentially vague terms like 'materially adverse' could lead to disputes
Restricts amendments that could be necessary for operational flexibility
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from amending its governing documents in a way that could adversely affect the Parent or delay the merger.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (b) split, reverse split, combine, subdivide, reclassify, redeem, repurchase or otherwise acquire or amend the terms of the Company's or any of its Subsidiaries' capital stock, or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests, or any options, warrants, convertible securities or other rights of any kind to acquire any shares of the Company's or any of its Subsidiaries' capital stock or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests; provided that the Company may repurchase or otherwise acquire shares in connection with (i) the acceptance of shares of Company Common Stock as payment for the per share exercise price of the Company Stock Options or as payment for Taxes incurred in connection with the exercise, vesting or settlement of Company Equity Awards, in each case in accordance with the applicable Company Equity Plan or (ii) the forfeiture of Company Equity Awards;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (c) issue, sell, pledge, dispose of, encumber, grant or authorize the same with respect to, any shares of the Company's or its Subsidiaries' capital stock, or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests, or any options, warrants, convertible securities or other rights of any kind to acquire any shares of the Company's or any of its Subsidiaries' capital stock or other equity or equity-based compensation, or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests; provided that the Company may issue the foregoing (i) upon the settlement of any Company Stock Option, Company RSU Award or Company PSU Award outstanding as of the date of this Agreement, (ii) pursuant to the terms of the Company ESPP in effect immediately prior to the date of this Agreement or (iii) to the extent permitted by Section 5.1(e);
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (c) issue, sell, pledge, dispose of, encumber, grant or authorize the same with respect to, any shares of the Company's or its Subsidiaries' capital stock, or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests, or any options, warrants, convertible securities or other rights of any kind to acquire any shares of the Company's or any of its Subsidiaries' capital stock or other equity or equity-based compensation, or other equity or voting securities or other equity interests; provided that the Company may issue the foregoing (i) upon the settlement of any the Company Stock Option, the Company RSU Award or the Company PSU Award outstanding as of the date of this Agreement, (ii) pursuant to the terms of the Company ESPP in effect immediately prior to the date of this Agreement or (iii) to the extent permitted by Section 5.1(e);

Permitted actions under the Company ESPP

assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from issuing or encumbering its capital stock or equity securities without buyer consent, except for specified exceptions.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (d) declare, set aside, authorize, make or pay any dividend or other distribution, payable in cash, stock, property or otherwise, with respect to the Company's or any of its Subsidiaries' capital stock or other equity interests, other than cash dividends and distributions paid by any direct or indirect wholly owned Subsidiary of the Company to the Company or any direct or indirect wholly owned Subsidiary of the Company;

seller_favorable_aspects: Permits cash dividends and distributions from wholly owned Subsidiaries to the Company or other wholly owned Subsidiaries
red_flags: Potentially limits the Company's ability to return capital to shareholders during the interim period

assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company and its Subsidiaries from declaring or paying any dividends or distributions, except for certain permitted distributions from wholly owned Subsidiaries.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (e) except to the extent required pursuant to any Company Benefit Plan as in effect on the date of this Agreement,

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (i) establish, adopt, enter into any new, amend, terminate or take any action to accelerate rights under, any Company Benefit Plan or plan, program, policy, practice, agreement or arrangement that would be a Company Benefit Plan if it had been in effect on the date of this Agreement;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (iv) take any action (other than actions contemplated by this Agreement) to accelerate any payment or benefit, the vesting of any equity or equity-based award or the funding of any payment or benefit, payable or to become payable to any current or former employee or director of, or individual service provider to, the Company or any Subsidiary of the Company;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (v) enter into any employment, severance, change in control, retention, individual consulting or similar agreement with any current or former employee or director of, or individual service provider to, the Company or any Subsidiary of the Company (other than offer letters in the ordinary course of business that provide for at-will employment (or employment, if at-will employment is not permitted by applicable Law in the relevant jurisdiction) without any severance, retention or change in control benefits for newly hired employees or individual service providers who are hired to the extent permitted by Section 5.1(f));

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (vi) communicate in writing with employees of the Company or any Subsidiary of the Company regarding the compensation, benefits or other treatment they will receive following the Effective Time, unless such communications are consistent with the terms provided herein;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (vi) communicate in writing with employees of the Company or any Subsidiary of the Company regarding the compensation, benefits or other treatment they will receive following the Effective Time, unless such communications are consistent with the terms provided herein;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Pre-Closing Operational Restrictions with Spin-Off and Asset-Specific Carveouts
red_flags: potentially limits necessary communication with employees
may create uncertainty among employees regarding their future
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company's ability to communicate with employees about their compensation and benefits post-merger unless such communications align with the agreement's terms.

outlier_analysis.why_unusual: This clause is unusual because it specifically restricts communication with employees regarding compensation and benefits post-merger, which is not a common focus in typical operational conduct covenants. Most operational covenants concentrate on broader operational activities, financial performance, or strategic decisions rather than detailed employee communication protocols.
specific_differences: Focus on employee communication rather than general operational conduct.
Emphasis on post-merger treatment of employees, which is typically not addressed in pre-closing operational restrictions.
Inclusion of a requirement for consistency with terms provided, indicating a specific alignment with pre-defined conditions rather than general operational guidelines.
outlier_analysis.comparison_to_cluster: Unlike typical operations_conduct covenants that generally address the conduct of business in terms of financial metrics, operational performance, or strategic initiatives, this clause zeroes in on employee-related communications, which is more specific and less common in pre-closing operational restrictions.
lawyer_should_review_for: Potential implications for employee morale and retention during the merger process.
Clarity and enforceability of the terms regarding what constitutes 'consistent' communication.
Risks of non-compliance with labor laws or regulations regarding employee communication and treatment.
unusual_elements: Specificity regarding employee communication about compensation and benefits.
Requirement for consistency with pre-defined terms, which adds a layer of complexity.
Focus on post-merger implications rather than just pre-merger operational conduct.

assessed_clauses.original_text: (vii) except as may be required by GAAP, materially change any actuarial or other assumptions used to calculate funding obligations with respect to any Company Benefit Plan, make any voluntary contributions to a Company Benefit Plan or materially change the manner in which contributions to such plans are made or the basis on which such contributions are determined;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (i) acquire (including by merger, consolidation or acquisition of stock or assets or otherwise) any corporation, partnership, limited liability company, joint venture, other business organization, business or assets of any other Person constituting a business or any portion of a business for consideration in excess of $25,000,000 in the aggregate;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (j) sell, pledge, dispose of, transfer, abandon, lease, license, mortgage, incur any Lien (including pursuant to a sale-leaseback transaction or an asset securitization transaction) on or otherwise transfer or encumber any portion of the tangible or intangible assets, business, properties or rights of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries (including any Company Lease or Company Owned Real Property) having a fair market value in excess of $5,000,000 individually or $25,000,000 in the aggregate, except (i) sales of inventory and accounts receivable in the ordinary course of business, (ii) transfers solely among the Company and its direct or indirect wholly owned Subsidiaries, (iii) dispositions of obsolete tangible assets or expired inventory, (iv) with respect to immaterial leases, licenses or other similar grants of real property, any immaterial grant, amendment, extension, modification, or renewal in the ordinary course of business, (v) Permitted Liens or (vi) non-exclusive licenses of Intellectual Property rights to customers or suppliers in their capacities as such in the ordinary course of business;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (o) make any material change to its methods of financial accounting, except as required by GAAP (or any interpretation thereof) or Regulation S-X of the Exchange Act;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (q) release, compromise, assign, settle or agree to settle any Proceeding (excluding (i) any Proceeding relating to Taxes, which shall be governed exclusively by Section 5.1(t), and (ii) any Proceeding governed by Section 5.18), other than settlements that result solely in monetary obligations of the Company or its Subsidiaries (without the admission of wrongdoing or a nolo contendere or similar plea, the imposition of injunctive or other equitable relief, or restrictions on the future activity or conduct by, of or on Parent, the Company or any of their respective Subsidiaries, except for confidentiality and similar de minimis obligations) involving payment by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries of an amount not greater than $2,500,000 individually or $10,000,000 in the aggregate;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (q) release, compromise, assign, settle or agree to settle any Proceeding (excluding (i) any Proceeding relating to Taxes, which shall be governed exclusively by Section 5.1(t), and (ii) any Proceeding governed by Section 5.18), other than settlements that result solely in monetary obligations of the Company or its Subsidiaries (without the admission of wrongdoing or a nolo contendere or similar plea, the imposition of injunctive or other equitable relief, or restrictions on the future activity or conduct by, of or on Parent, the Company or any of their respective Subsidiaries, except for confidentiality and similar de minimis obligations) involving payment by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries of an amount not greater than $2,500,000 individually or $10,000,000 in the aggregate;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Monetary Thresholds for Settling Legal Proceedings and Actions
seller_favorable_aspects: Monetary threshold for settlements allows for flexibility up to $2,500,000 individually and $10,000,000 in the aggregate without buyer consent.
red_flags: Restrictions on settlements could limit the company's ability to resolve legal issues efficiently.
Exclusions for certain types of proceedings may create ambiguity in enforcement.
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company's ability to settle legal proceedings without buyer consent if the settlement involves non-monetary obligations or exceeds specified monetary thresholds.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (r) fail to use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain in effect the existing material insurance policies covering the Company and its Subsidiaries and their respective properties, assets and businesses;

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (t) (i) make, change or revoke any material Tax election; (ii) file any material amendment to a material Tax Return; (iii) settle, consent to or compromise any audit or Proceeding with respect to a material amount of Taxes for an amount materially in excess of the amount reserved therefor; (iv) agree to an extension or waiver of the statute of limitations with respect to any material Taxes (other than in connection with extensions to file Tax Returns obtained in the ordinary course); (v) enter into any "closing agreement" within the meaning of Section 7121 of the Code (or any similar provision of state, local or non-U.S. Law) with respect to a material amount of Tax; (vi) surrender any right to claim a material Tax refund, offset or other reduction in Tax liability; (vii) change any material aspect of its method of Tax accounting; (viii) change any annual Tax accounting period; or (ix) request any material Tax ruling;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (t) (i) make, change or revoke any material Tax election; (ii) file any material amendment to a material Tax Return; (iii) settle, consent to or compromise any audit or Proceeding with respect to a material amount of Taxes for an amount materially in excess of the amount reserved therefor; (iv) agree to an extension or waiver of the statute of limitations with respect to any material Taxes (other than in connection with extensions to file Tax Returns obtained in the ordinary course); (v) enter into any "closing agreement" within the meaning of Section 7121 of the Code (or any similar provision of state, local or non-U.S. Law) with respect to a material amount of Tax; (vi) surrender any right to claim a material Tax refund, offset or other reduction in Tax liability; (vii) change any material aspect of its method of Tax accounting; (viii) change any annual Tax accounting period; or (ix) request any material Tax ruling;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Restrictions on Tax Elections, Returns, and Administrative Actions
seller_favorable_aspects: materiality thresholds
ordinary course exceptions for filing extensions
red_flags: potential for significant operational constraints
risk of unexpected tax liabilities due to restrictions
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the company from making significant changes to its tax positions or filings without buyer consent, which could impact its financial standing.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (u) knowingly take any action (other than an action expressly contemplated or required under this Agreement) or knowingly fail to take any action (other than an action prohibited by this Agreement) with the knowledge that such action or failure to act could reasonably be expected to cause Parent to be treated as a "surrogate foreign corporation" within the meaning of Section 7874(a)(2)(B) of the Code as a result of the Merger;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (u) knowingly take any action (other than an action expressly contemplated or required under this Agreement) or knowingly fail to take any action (other than an action prohibited by this Agreement) with the knowledge that such action or failure to act could reasonably be expected to cause Parent to be treated as a "surrogate foreign corporation" within the meaning of Section 7874(a)(2)(B) of the Code as a result of the Merger;

red_flags: Potentially broad interpretation of actions and omissions
Risk of triggering tax implications under Section 7874
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from taking or failing to take actions that could lead to it being classified as a 'surrogate foreign corporation' for tax purposes.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (v) merge or consolidate the Company or any of its Subsidiaries with any Person or adopt a plan of complete or partial liquidation, dissolution, recapitalization or other reorganization of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (v) merge or consolidate the Company or any of its Subsidiaries with any Person or adopt a plan of complete or partial liquidation, dissolution, recapitalization or other reorganization of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Prohibition on Liquidation, Dissolution, Restructuring and Fundamental Corporate Changes
red_flags: absolute prohibition on mergers and consolidations
no exceptions for strategic transactions
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company and its Subsidiaries from merging, consolidating, or undergoing any form of reorganization.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (w) implement any material reduction in force program with respect to the full time salaried employees of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries outside the ordinary course of business;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (w) implement any material reduction in force program with respect to the full time salaried employees of the Company or any of its Subsidiaries outside the ordinary course of business;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Restrictions on Implementing New Policies and Strategic Operational Changes
seller_favorable_aspects: The clause allows for reductions in force only outside the ordinary course of business, which provides some flexibility for the seller.
red_flags: The definition of 'ordinary course of business' may be subjective, potentially leading to disputes over what constitutes a material reduction.
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from implementing any significant layoffs of full-time salaried employees outside of normal business operations.

outlier_analysis.why_unusual: This clause is unusual because it specifically targets a material reduction in force, which is a significant operational change, rather than broader strategic or policy changes typically covered in similar covenants. The specificity of 'full-time salaried employees' and the restriction on actions 'outside the ordinary course of business' adds a level of granularity not commonly found in standard operational conduct covenants.
specific_differences: Focus on employee reductions rather than general operational changes or policy implementations.
Inclusion of a specific employee category ('full-time salaried employees') which narrows the scope of the covenant.
Explicit mention of 'ordinary course of business' which is not always a standard phrase in similar covenants.
outlier_analysis.comparison_to_cluster: Unlike typical operations_conduct covenants that may broadly restrict changes in policies or strategies, this clause is narrowly focused on workforce reductions, which may not align with the general intent of operational conduct restrictions that often encompass a wider range of strategic decisions.
lawyer_should_review_for: Potential implications for employee relations and morale if reductions are perceived as imminent or likely.
Clarity on what constitutes 'ordinary course of business' to avoid ambiguity in enforcement.
Compliance with labor laws and regulations related to layoffs and workforce reductions.
unusual_elements: Specificity regarding the type of employee affected (full-time salaried).
Direct limitation on workforce reduction as a standalone operational change.
The phrase 'outside the ordinary course of business' which may create interpretative challenges.

assessed_clauses.section_title: Conduct of Business by the Company Pending the Merger
assessed_clauses.original_text: (x) implement any closure of a facility that is material to the operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole; or
assessed_clauses.processed_text: (x) implement any closure of a facility that is material to the operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole; or
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Restrictions on Implementing New Policies and Strategic Operational Changes
seller_favorable_aspects: materiality threshold allows for some operational flexibility
red_flags: potential for significant operational constraints
risk of impacting business continuity
assessed_clauses.covenant_explanation: This covenant restricts the Company from closing any facility that is material to its overall operations without consent.

outlier_analysis.why_unusual: This clause is unusual because it specifically targets the closure of facilities, which is a more concrete and operationally focused restriction compared to the broader and more abstract nature of typical covenants that address policy changes or strategic operational shifts. Most covenants in this category tend to allow for some level of operational flexibility, whereas this clause imposes a strict prohibition on a specific action that could significantly impact the company's operations.
specific_differences: The clause explicitly mentions the closure of a facility, which is a specific operational action, rather than general policy changes or strategic decisions.
It uses the term 'material to the operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries,' which introduces a threshold of significance that is not commonly found in standard operational covenants.
This clause lacks the typical language that allows for exceptions or conditions under which operational changes can be made, making it more restrictive.
outlier_analysis.comparison_to_cluster: Unlike typical operations_conduct covenants that allow for a range of strategic operational changes and often include provisions for exceptions or conditions, this clause is narrowly focused on prohibiting a specific action (closure of a facility) without room for flexibility or exceptions.
lawyer_should_review_for: Potential implications of facility closures on the company's operational capacity and financial health.
Whether the clause aligns with the overall strategic objectives of the merger and if it could hinder necessary operational adjustments.
The definition of 'material' and how it could be interpreted in practice, which may lead to disputes.
unusual_elements: Materiality threshold that may complicate operational decisions.
Lack of flexibility or exceptions for operational changes.

assessed_clauses.original_text: (y) enter into any Contract to do, authorize or adopt any resolutions approving, or announce an intention to do, any of the foregoing.
